AbsolutePathBuf.description:
A path that is guaranteed to be absolute and normalized (though it is not
guaranteed to be canonicalized or exist on the filesystem).

IMPORTANT: When deserializing an `AbsolutePathBuf`, a base path must be set
using [AbsolutePathBufGuard::new]. If no base path is set, the
deserialization will fail unless the path being deserialized is already
absolute.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to no limit.

senderLabel.description: Claimed sender label for attribution only.

senderThreadId.description:
Claimed sender thread for attribution only. activeSession/send does not
treat this as authentication or authorization.

targetPeerId.description: Active peer routing key from activeSession/list.

targetThreadId.description:
Legacy local Codewith-thread target. target_peer_id is preferred for
peers returned by activeSession/list.

source.description:
Optional provenance for client-supplied context from a local app store.

This metadata is for clients and auditability. It does not change
model-visible content or trust: `kind` remains the authority for whether
`value` is injected as untrusted user context or application context.

id.description: Stable record id inside the source namespace.

label.description: Optional user-facing label for rendering/editing the context source.

namespace.description:
Stable source namespace, for example `open-projects`, `open-todos`,
`open-conversations`, or `open-mementos`.

recordType.description:
Optional source record kind, for example `project`, `task`,
`conversation`, or `memento`.

read.description: This will be removed in favor of `entries`.

write.description: This will be removed in favor of `entries`.

ApprovalsReviewer.description: Configures who approval requests are routed to for review. Examples include sandbox escapes, blocked network access, MCP approval prompts, and ARC escalations. Defaults to `user`. `auto_review` uses a carefully prompted subagent to gather relevant context and apply a risk-based decision framework before approving or denying the request. The legacy value `guardian_subagent` is accepted for compatibility.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

forceRefetch.description: When true, bypass app caches and fetch the latest data from sources.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

threadId.description: Optional thread id used to evaluate app feature gating from that thread's config.

CollaborationMode.description: Collaboration mode for a Codewith session.

CommandExecParams.description:
Run a standalone command (argv vector) in the server sandbox without
creating a thread or turn.

The final `command/exec` response is deferred until the process exits and is
sent only after all `command/exec/outputDelta` notifications for that
connection have been emitted.

command.description: Command argv vector. Empty arrays are rejected.

cwd.description: Optional working directory. Defaults to the server cwd.

disableOutputCap.description:
Disable stdout/stderr capture truncation for this request.

Cannot be combined with `outputBytesCap`.

disableTimeout.description:
Disable the timeout entirely for this request.

Cannot be combined with `timeoutMs`.

env.description:
Optional environment overrides merged into the server-computed
environment.

Matching names override inherited values. Set a key to `null` to unset
an inherited variable.

outputBytesCap.description:
Optional per-stream stdout/stderr capture cap in bytes.

When omitted, the server default applies. Cannot be combined with
`disableOutputCap`.

processId.description:
Optional client-supplied, connection-scoped process id.

Required for `tty`, `streamStdin`, `streamStdoutStderr`, and follow-up
`command/exec/write`, `command/exec/resize`, and
`command/exec/terminate` calls. When omitted, buffered execution gets an
internal id that is not exposed to the client.

sandboxPolicy.description:
Optional sandbox policy for this command.

Uses the same shape as thread/turn execution sandbox configuration and
defaults to the user's configured policy when omitted. Cannot be
combined with `permissionProfile`.

size.description:
Optional initial PTY size in character cells. Only valid when `tty` is
true.

streamStdin.description:
Allow follow-up `command/exec/write` requests to write stdin bytes.

Requires a client-supplied `processId`.

streamStdoutStderr.description:
Stream stdout/stderr via `command/exec/outputDelta` notifications.

Streamed bytes are not duplicated into the final response and require a
client-supplied `processId`.

timeoutMs.description:
Optional timeout in milliseconds.

When omitted, the server default applies. Cannot be combined with
`disableTimeout`.

tty.description:
Enable PTY mode.

This implies `streamStdin` and `streamStdoutStderr`.

processId.description:
Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original
`command/exec` request.

size.description: New PTY size in character cells.

CommandExecTerminalSize.description: PTY size in character cells for `command/exec` PTY sessions.

processId.description:
Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original
`command/exec` request.

CommandExecWriteParams.description:
Write stdin bytes to a running `command/exec` session, close stdin, or
both.

closeStdin.description: Close stdin after writing `deltaBase64`, if present.

deltaBase64.description: Optional base64-encoded stdin bytes to write.

processId.description:
Client-supplied, connection-scoped `processId` from the original
`command/exec` request.

oneOf.description: User approved the command.

User approved the command and future prompts in the same session-scoped
approval cache should run without prompting.

User approved the command, and wants to apply the proposed execpolicy amendment so future
matching commands can run without prompting.
oneOf.title: AcceptWithExecpolicyAmendmentCommandExecutionApprovalDecision

oneOf.description: User chose a persistent network policy rule (allow/deny) for this host.
oneOf.title: ApplyNetworkPolicyAmendmentCommandExecutionApprovalDecision

oneOf.description: User denied the command. The agent will continue the turn.
User denied the command. The turn will also be immediately interrupted.

filePath.description: Path to the config file to write; defaults to the user's `config.toml` when omitted.

reloadUserConfig.description: When true, hot-reload the updated user config into all loaded threads after writing.

cwd.description:
Optional working directory to resolve project config layers. If specified,
return the effective config as seen from that directory (i.e., including any
project layers between `cwd` and the project/repo root).

filePath.description: Path to the config file to write; defaults to the user's `config.toml` when omitted.

authProfile.description:
Auth profile used for this reset. `null` selects the default root auth;
omission leaves the app-server's selected auth profile unchanged.

creditId.description:
Opaque reset-credit identifier to redeem. When omitted, the backend selects the next
available credit.

expectedAccountIdentityFingerprint.description: Required opaque account fingerprint from the read that selected this reset credit.

idempotencyKey.description:
Identifies one logical reset attempt. A UUID is recommended; reuse the same value when
retrying that attempt.

enablement.description:
Process-wide runtime feature enablement keyed by canonical feature name.

Only named features are updated. Omitted features are left unchanged.
Send an empty map for a no-op.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

threadId.description:
Optional loaded thread id. Pass this when showing feature state for an
existing thread so enablement is computed from that thread's refreshed
config, including project-local config for the thread's cwd.

cwds.description: Zero or more working directories to include for repo-scoped detection.

includeHome.description: If true, include detection under the user's home (~/.claude, ~/.codewith, etc.).

cwd.description: Null or empty means home-scoped migration; non-empty means repo-scoped migration.

oneOf.description: User approved the file changes.
User approved the file changes and future changes to the same files should run without prompting.
User denied the file changes. The agent will continue the turn.
User denied the file changes. The turn will also be immediately interrupted.

FsCopyParams.description: Copy a file or directory tree on the host filesystem.

recursive.description: Required for directory copies; ignored for file copies.

FsCreateDirectoryParams.description: Create a directory on the host filesystem.

recursive.description: Whether parent directories should also be created. Defaults to `true`.

FsGetMetadataParams.description: Request metadata for an absolute path.

FsReadDirectoryParams.description: List direct child names for a directory.

FsReadFileParams.description: Read a file from the host filesystem.

FsRemoveParams.description:
Remove a file or directory tree from the host filesystem.

The target must resolve inside the session's authorized workspace scope; the
server rejects paths (including symlink escapes) outside every workspace root.

force.description:
Whether missing paths should be ignored. Defaults to `false`; ignoring
missing targets must be requested explicitly.

path.description: Absolute path to remove. Must be inside an authorized workspace root.

recursive.description:
Whether directory removal should recurse. Defaults to `false`; recursive
tree deletion must be requested explicitly.

FsUnwatchParams.description: Stop filesystem watch notifications for a prior `fs/watch`.

FsWatchParams.description: Start filesystem watch notifications for an absolute path.

path.description: Absolute file or directory path to watch.

watchId.description: Connection-scoped watch identifier used for `fs/unwatch` and `fs/changed`.

FsWriteFileParams.description: Write a file on the host filesystem.

FunctionCallOutputContentItem.description:
Responses API compatible content items that can be returned by a tool call.
This is a subset of ContentItem with the types we support as function call outputs.

refreshToken.description:
When `true`, requests a proactive token refresh before returning.

In managed auth mode this triggers the normal refresh-token flow. In
external auth mode this flag is ignored. Clients should refresh tokens
themselves and call `account/login/start` with `chatgptAuthTokens`.

authProfile.description:
Auth profile used for this read. `null` selects the default root auth;
omission leaves the app-server's selected auth profile unchanged.

includeResetCreditDetails.description: Include detailed reset-credit rows even when no credits remain available.

cwds.description: When empty, defaults to the current session working directory.

experimentalApi.description: Opt into receiving experimental API methods and fields.

optOutNotificationMethods.description:
Exact notification method names that should be suppressed for this
connection (for example `thread/started`).

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

detail.description:
Controls how much MCP inventory data to fetch for each server.
Defaults to `Full` when omitted.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a server-defined value.

oneOf.description:
[UNSTABLE] FOR OPENAI INTERNAL USE ONLY - DO NOT USE.
The access token must contain the same scopes that Codewith-managed ChatGPT auth tokens have.

accessToken.description:
Access token (JWT) supplied by the client.
This token is used for backend API requests and email extraction.

chatgptAccountId.description: Workspace/account identifier supplied by the client.

chatgptPlanType.description:
Optional plan type supplied by the client.

When `null`, Codewith attempts to derive the plan type from access-token
claims. If unavailable, the plan defaults to `unknown`.

oneOf.title: ChatgptAuthTokensLoginAccountParams

MessagePhase.description:
Classifies an assistant message as interim commentary or final answer text.

Providers do not emit this consistently, so callers must treat `None` as
"phase unknown" and keep compatibility behavior for legacy models.

oneOf.description:
Mid-turn assistant text (for example preamble/progress narration).

Additional tool calls or assistant output may follow before turn
completion.
The assistant's terminal answer text for the current turn.

ModeKind.description: Initial collaboration mode to use when the TUI starts.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

includeHidden.description: When true, include models that are hidden from the default picker list.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

modelGateway.description: Gateway id to scope model browsing. Omission preserves active/provider behavior.

modelProvider.description: Provider id whose models should be listed. Omission uses the active provider.

upstreamProvider.description: Aggregator upstream provider id, such as the prefix in OpenRouter model slugs.

modelGateway.description: Gateway id to scope provider browsing. Omission preserves the provider-only catalog.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

cwd.description: Optional working directory to resolve project config layers.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to the full result set.

cwds.description: Optional working directories used to discover repo marketplaces.

installSuggestionPluginNames.description:
Additional uninstalled plugin names that should be returned when present locally.
This is used by mention surfaces that intentionally expose install entrypoints.

cwds.description:
Optional working directories used to discover repo marketplaces. When omitted,
only home-scoped marketplaces and the official curated marketplace are considered.

marketplaceKinds.description:
Optional marketplace kind filter. When omitted, only local marketplaces are queried, plus
the default remote catalog when enabled by feature flag.

ProcessTerminalSize.description: PTY size in character cells for `process/spawn` PTY sessions.

ReasoningEffort.description: A non-empty reasoning effort value advertised by the model.

ReasoningSummary.description:
A summary of the reasoning performed by the model. This can be useful for
debugging and understanding the model's reasoning process.
See https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/reasoning?api-mode=responses#reasoning-summaries

oneOf.description: Option to disable reasoning summaries.

call_id.description: Set when using the Responses API.

id.description: Legacy id field retained for compatibility with older payloads.

delivery.description:
Where to run the review: inline (default) on the current thread or
detached on a new thread (returned in `reviewThreadId`).

oneOf.description: Review the working tree: staged, unstaged, and untracked files.
oneOf.title: UncommittedChangesReviewTarget

oneOf.description: Review changes between the current branch and the given base branch.
oneOf.title: BaseBranchReviewTarget

oneOf.description: Review the changes introduced by a specific commit.

title.description: Optional human-readable label (e.g., commit subject) for UIs.

oneOf.title: CommitReviewTarget

oneOf.description: Arbitrary instructions, equivalent to the old free-form prompt.
oneOf.title: CustomReviewTarget

oneOf.description:
Use the current shared checkout and disallow session-managed worktree
create/attach operations for this thread.
Allow this session to manually create or attach managed worktrees.

Pull-request mode. The session must work inside an attached isolated
managed worktree before model turns can continue.

cwds.description: When empty, defaults to the current session working directory.

forceReload.description: When true, bypass the skills cache and re-scan skills from disk.

byteRange.description: Byte range in the parent `text` buffer that this element occupies.

placeholder.description: Optional human-readable placeholder for the element, displayed in the UI.

ThreadForkParams.description:
There are two ways to fork a thread:
1. By thread_id: load the thread from disk by thread_id and fork it into a new thread.
2. By path: load the thread from disk by path and fork it into a new thread.

If using a non-empty path, the thread_id param will be ignored.
Empty string path values are treated as absent.

Prefer using thread_id whenever possible.

approvalsReviewer.description:
Override where approval requests are routed for review on this thread
and subsequent turns.

authProfile.description:
Auth profile selected for model requests on this thread. `null` selects
the default root auth; omission leaves config/env selection unchanged.

model.description: Configuration overrides for the forked thread, if any.

threadSource.description: Optional client-supplied analytics source classification for this forked thread.

items.description: Raw Responses API items to append to the thread's model-visible history.

archived.description:
Optional archived filter; when set to true, only archived threads are returned.
If false or null, only non-archived threads are returned.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

cwd.description:
Optional cwd filter or filters; when set, only threads whose session cwd
exactly matches one of these paths are returned.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to a reasonable server-side value.

modelProviders.description:
Optional provider filter; when set, only sessions recorded under these
providers are returned. When present but empty, includes all providers.

searchTerm.description: Optional substring filter for the extracted thread title.

sortDirection.description: Optional sort direction; defaults to descending (newest first).

sortKey.description: Optional sort key; defaults to created_at.

sourceKinds.description:
Optional source filter; when set, only sessions from these source kinds
are returned. When omitted or empty, defaults to interactive sources.

useStateDbOnly.description:
If true, return from the state DB without scanning JSONL rollouts to
repair thread metadata. Omitted or false preserves scan-and-repair
behavior.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to no limit.

branch.description:
Omit to leave the stored branch unchanged, set to `null` to clear it,
or provide a non-empty string to replace it.

originUrl.description:
Omit to leave the stored origin URL unchanged, set to `null` to clear it,
or provide a non-empty string to replace it.

sha.description:
Omit to leave the stored commit unchanged, set to `null` to clear it,
or provide a non-empty string to replace it.

gitInfo.description:
Patch the stored Git metadata for this thread.
Omit a field to leave it unchanged, set it to `null` to clear it, or
provide a string to replace the stored value.

cwd.description:
Optional working directory relative to the thread cwd. Parent directory
traversal and absolute paths are rejected.

outputFile.description:
Optional output file relative to the monitor cwd. Parent directory
traversal, absolute paths, and symlink targets are rejected.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned by a previous call.

limit.description: Optional page size; defaults to no limit.

includeTurns.description: When true, include turns and their items from rollout history.

ThreadRealtimeStartTransport.description: EXPERIMENTAL - transport used by thread realtime.

sdp.description:
SDP offer generated by a WebRTC RTCPeerConnection after configuring audio and the
realtime events data channel.

itemsView.description: How much item detail to include for each returned turn; defaults to summary.

sortDirection.description: Optional turn pagination direction; defaults to descending.

ThreadResumeParams.description:
There are three ways to resume a thread:
1. By thread_id: load the thread from disk by thread_id and resume it.
2. By history: instantiate the thread from memory and resume it.
3. By path: load the thread from disk by path and resume it.

For non-running threads, the precedence is: history > non-empty path > thread_id.
If using history or a non-empty path for a non-running thread, the thread_id
param will be ignored.

If thread_id identifies a running thread, app-server rejoins that thread and
treats a non-empty path as a consistency check against the active rollout path.
Empty string path values are treated as absent.

Prefer using thread_id whenever possible.

approvalsReviewer.description:
Override where approval requests are routed for review on this thread
and subsequent turns.

authProfile.description:
Auth profile selected for model requests on this thread. `null` selects
the default root auth; omission leaves config/env selection unchanged.

model.description: Configuration overrides for the resumed thread, if any.

numTurns.description:
The number of turns to drop from the end of the thread. Must be >= 1.

This only modifies the thread's history and does not revert local file changes
that have been made by the agent. Clients are responsible for reverting these changes.

command.description:
Shell command string evaluated by the thread's configured shell.
Unlike `command/exec`, this intentionally preserves shell syntax
such as pipes, redirects, and quoting. This runs unsandboxed with full
access rather than inheriting the thread sandbox policy.

approvalsReviewer.description:
Override where approval requests are routed for review on this thread
and subsequent turns.

authProfile.description:
Auth profile selected for model requests on this thread. `null` selects
the default root auth; omission leaves config/env selection unchanged.

parentThreadId.description: Optional parent thread id when starting a fresh subagent child thread.

threadSource.description: Optional client-supplied analytics source classification for this thread.

ToolRequestUserInputAnswer.description: EXPERIMENTAL. Captures a user's answer to a request_user_input question.

oneOf.description: `items` was not loaded for this turn. The field is intentionally empty.
`items` contains only a display summary for this turn.
`items` contains every ThreadItem available from persisted app-server history for this turn.

approvalPolicy.description: Override the approval policy for this turn and subsequent turns.

Override where approval requests are routed for review on this turn and
subsequent turns.

serviceTier.description: Override the service tier for this turn and subsequent turns.

expectedTurnId.description:
Required active turn id precondition. The request fails when it does not
match the currently active turn.

text_elements.description: UI-defined spans within `text` used to render or persist special elements.
